description_html:
Objective:

  • Design and build a wireless, manually operated, remotely controlled boat that must navigate through obstacles within the least amount of time.

Team structure:

  • A team can consist of 1 to 4 members including a team leader.
  • Communications, if any, will be made to the team leader’s contact number or email address.
  • All members are required to possess a valid ID card from their respective institution.
  • Cross-department teams are allowed.
  • Cross-college teams are not allowed.
  • School students are allowed with a valid pass.
  • All teams must compulsorily register on Unstop and must possess a valid fest pass to participate in the final round of the event.

Stage 1:

  • All registered teams are required to submit a brief report on Unstop, stating their boat’s features and specifications that are capable of fulfilling the technical requirements for the competition.
  • This report shall be no more than 2 pages in length. The shorter the report, the better.
  • There is no specific format for the report. Those entries with innovative designs that satisfy the requirements will be shortlisted and the corresponding teams shall be notified.

Stage 2:

  • This is a timed challenge, where the boat is to be maneuvered through a series of obstacles in the arena.
  • A size and safety check will be carried out for each team’s boat that attends the event after being shortlisted for stage 2.
  • If there are more than 10 shortlisted teams, stage 2 will feature 2 rounds:
    • Round 1 - this is an elimination round where teams shall be paired up to compete against each other in a simple race, with each boat starting from opposite ends of the pool. The teams whose boats reach the opposite corner in the fastest time, advance to round 2. The number of teams selected for the next round shall be fixed by
      the organizers.
    • Round 2 - each team shall be given time slots to deploy their boats and navigate through the obstacles within 8 minutes. For round 1, teams will be provided with 20 seconds to set up their boats before starting the race. For round 2, Teams will be provided 1 minute to study the arena and set up their boat before performing their tasks. At the end of 1 minute, the timer will start, and any delays in setup of the boat will be accounted for during the allotted time of 8 minutes.
  • The Arena:
    • The arena is a pool of size 400*200*100 cm.
    • The arena consists of a maze that is to be navigated from start to finish. The minimum width of the passage at any point of the obstacle is at least 40 cm.
    • For round 2, the boat must carry balls in its hull and navigate through the obstacl course. The ball is about 4 cm in diameter and weighs no more than 3
      grams. The balls will be provided by the organizers. The more balls a boat carries at a time successfully, the more points it receives.
    • A team is considered to have completed the maze successfully if it maneuvers the boat from start to finish barring a maximum total of 3 of any penalty moves, which include wall/pool bottom touches, and obstacle prop touches.
    • If a team completes the maze within the time limit, it is encouraged to try the
      maze in the reverse direction, i.e. from finish point to start point.

The top view of the arena resembles a schematic as follows:

  • Points scoring system (for round 2):
  • Each team gets exactly 2 attempts to complete the obstacle course. If both attempts are made, the best attempt will be considered for evaluation.
  • Completing the obstacle course once successfully without any ball receives 50
    points.
  • For each ball carried in the boat at every attempt, 10 points will be given.
  • If the boat touches any obstacle props, -3 points is given as a penalty.
  • Boats touching the walls or bottom of the pool receive -5 points.
  • If any boat makes more than 3 penalty moves, it will have to restart from the beginning.
  • In case of any unexpected failure of the boat in the midst of deployment, a technical timeout is allowed, where the timer will be stopped for a maximum of 60 seconds only. This will be provided only once in the entire time duration and at one stretch. This timeout is to be requested by the team leader or the controller of the boat in order to be granted. If the boat stops or fails for a second time, the team will be disqualified from continuing the task.

Technical requirements:

  • The boat should fit within a box of dimensions 40*20*15 cm.
  • Boats that fit within a box of dimensions 20*15*10 cm receive a bonus of 5 points.
  • External power supply to the boat is strictly forbidden. Teams are allowed only to use onboard power supplies for their boats.
  • The boat may be maneuvered using a tether that sends only control signals to the boat, in case an RC boat is not deployed. However, any tasks done with a tethered boat will score 10 points less than when performed with an RC boat.
  • The boat must NOT contain any harmful, corrosive or explosive materials.
  • The boat must not be made of any readymade kits like Lego kits. If found, the team shall be disqualified irrespective of the stage of the competition.
  • The boat, while in operation, must not harm other boats present in the arena, if any. It must not physically damage any part of the obstacle course or the pool.
  • It is recommended that the boat have a stop switch to stop/turn off the boat during times of emergency or when the boat goes out of control.

Other rules:

  • The order in which the teams are called for deploying their boats is solely the decision of the organizers, any request for changes will not be entertained. Teams appearing for stage 2 of the competition have to report well in time at the venue irrespective of whether they are called out first or not. Teams who will be arriving late due to unexpected circumstances must inform the organizers at least 24 hours before the start time of the event. Any other time delay requests shall not be entertained.
  • The time measured and the results declared by the organizers shall be considered final and binding.
  • In case of any disputes or discrepancies, the verdict given by the organizers shall be final and binding.
  • The exact schematic of the obstacle track might differ slightly in dimensions from what has been displayed. However, the technical requirements of the boats shall be followed strictly for the maximum allowable limit.
  • Any changes to the rules or further communication after Stage 1 selection will be communicated to the teams in writing.

Important dates:

  • Last date for registration: 10/03/2026
  • Last date for report submission: 15/03/2026
  • Competition: 28-29/03/2026
  • Prizes and Certificates:
  • First place - Rs. 12000
  • Second place - Rs. 8000
  • Third place - Rs. 5000
  • All participants will receive an e-certificate of participation.
